title: Ability to format text in work forms
excerpt: I have been trying to use workforms within a board to send to colleagues. There needs to be a description along with bullet points towards the top, but there no option to format the text and just ends up being bunched to…

posts.author: Ng84 (Nikki Gearing)
posts.cookedHtml: I have been trying to use workforms within a board to send to colleagues. There needs to be a description along with bullet points towards the top, but there no option to format the text and just ends up being bunched together in one paragraph.

posts.author: Nachliel (Nachliel from Easyapps)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Ng84

The monday form description supports HTML, allowing you to create bullet point lists with the ul and li tag’s. For example:

<ul>
  <li>Point A</li>
  <li>Point B</li>
  <li>Point C</li>
</ul>

Will be dispalyed as

  • Point A
  • Point B
  • Point C

In addition to supporting HTML in the form description, our monday form builder app Easyform supports this in the description of each question in the form, and on the Thank you page.

If you’re interested in creating styled forms that better match your brand and embed perfectly on your web page, you can check out our monday app Easyform and our extensive documentation site .

You can set up a free demo here or contact us at support@easyappshq.com.

posts.author: JenefaJ (Jenefa)
posts.cookedHtml:
You’re right — monday workforms themselves are quite limited when it comes to formatting. Text blocks don’t allow for styling, headings, or bullet points, so everything ends up in one paragraph.

With The PDF Maker’s Form Builder inside monday, you can get around this limitation:

  • Add introductory descriptions with proper spacing and formatting.

  • Include bullet points or numbered lists at the top so instructions are clear.

  • Collect responses directly into your board items or subitems.

  • Even add secure eSignatures if needed.

  • Automatically generate a polished PDF of responses for sharing with colleagues.

This way, your forms look more professional, are easier to read, and you can still keep everything tied to your monday boards.

Here’s the full guide: How to use the forms feature in monday
